questions: You'd rather stay content with what you have, than chase after bigger or better things. You're sorting through your email inbox on a Monday morning. Describe how you start your week.

You'd rather stay content with what you have, than chase after bigger or better things. You're organizing your desk at the end of the day. Describe what's on your mind.
You'd rather stay content with what you have, than chase after bigger or better things. A neighbor asks how work has been lately while you're both getting your mail.
You'd rather stay content with what you have, than chase after bigger or better things. You're reviewing your monthly budget on a quiet Saturday morning.
You'd rather stay content with what you have, than chase after bigger or better things. A friend you haven't seen in a while asks what you've been up to.
You'd rather stay content with what you have, than chase after bigger or better things. Your manager asks in a one-on-one how you're feeling about your current role.
You'd rather stay content with what you have, than chase after bigger or better things. You're helping a friend update their resume and start thinking about your own.
You'd rather stay content with what you have, than chase after bigger or better things. At a family dinner, your aunt asks if anything new is happening at work.
You'd rather stay content with what you have, than chase after bigger or better things. You overhear two strangers on the bus talking about career changes.
